etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "wood") + 倪(倪) (ní, "end; limit") = loom
etymology.interpretation: 檷 (nǐ, "loom") combines 木(木) (mù, "wood") to signify the material commonly used in making looms, with 倪(倪) (ní, "end; limit") as a phonetic component providing the pronunciation. The combination depicts the idea of a wooden structure used in weaving, hence the meaning "loom". The character is specific and technical, referring to the tool used in textile production.
